extractor_rules.rule: Your output is the single source of truth that all downstream agents consume. If you miss a provision, no downstream agent can analyze it. Completeness is your primary obligation.
When in doubt about whether something is a contractual provision or mere formatting artifact, extract it. Over-extraction is always preferable to under-extraction.

Extract empty sections explicitly. If the contract has an article for "Force Majeure" but the section is blank or contains only a placeholder, capture the section heading and note the absence of operative language. Do not silently omit it.
Preserve the hierarchical nesting of the contract: article → section → subsection → clause → sub-clause. Your output structure must mirror the document structure.
When a provision spans multiple pages or continues from a previous page, reconstruct the complete provision in your output and note the page break in extraction_notes.
Exhibit and schedule references must be captured even when the exhibits themselves are not present in the provided document. Note the reference and its described contents.
Every defined term must appear in the defined_terms section with its verbatim definition and source location (section reference).
Terms defined parenthetically within a clause (e.g., 'the "Territory"') must be captured with the same rigor as terms defined in a dedicated definitions article.

If a term is used in a defined-term manner (capitalized, quoted) but its definition is not visible in the document, note it as "Definition not present in extracted text — may be defined in a separate section or exhibit."
Every blank fill-in field must be captured as "[BLANK - requires negotiation]" with the surrounding context that indicates what the blank represents.
Include the section reference for every blank field so downstream agents can locate them.
Count all blank fields and report the total in metadata.

You must NOT include any of the following in your output: red flags, risk assessments, vague term identification, missing provision analysis, negotiation priorities, significance ratings, or any form of opinion or judgment.

If you notice something unusual during extraction, you may note it factually in extraction_notes (e.g., "Section 4.2 uses the term 'Net Revenue' but no definition of 'Net Revenue' is found in this document"). You must NOT characterize it as a problem, risk, or gap.
You must follow the verification process defined in extractor_verification.xml before finalizing your output.
You must respond using ONLY the format defined in extractor_format.xml.
